sections.title: Part 1
sections.passage: You will hear a conversation about renting furniture for a new house.

questionGroups.title: Furniture Rental Companies
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

rows.question: Prices range from $105 to $______ per room per month

rows.text:
Delivers in 1-2 days
Special offer: free

rows.question: free ______ with every living room set

rows.text: Offers cheapest prices for renting furniture and

rows.question: See the ______ for the most up-to-date prices

sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear an archaeologist called Hayden talking to visitors about the Bidcaster Community Archaeology Project.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: Who was responsible for starting the community project?

options.text: the castle owners
a national charity
the local council

questions.question: How was the gold coin found?

options.text: Heavy rain had removed some of the soil.
The ground was dug up by wild rabbits.
A person with a metal detector searched the area.

questions.question: What led the archaeologists to believe there was an ancient village on this site?

options.text: the lucky discovery of old records
the bases of several structures visible in the grass
the unusual stones found near the castle

questions.question: What are the team still hoping to find?

options.text: brooches
bone tools
jewellery

questions.question: What was found on the other side of the river to the castle?

options.text: an ancient field system
a long wall
some huts

questions.question: What do the team plan to do after work ends this summer?

options.text: put on a TV documentary
contribute to a museum exhibition
organise guided tours for school groups

questionGroups.instructions: Label the map below. Choose the correct letter, A–G.

questions.question: bridge foundations
rubbish pit
meeting hall
fish pond

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two students, Finn and Maya, discussing their project on theatre programmes.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: Finn was pleased to discover that their topic

options.text: was not familiar to their module leader.
had not been chosen by other students.
did not prove to be difficult to research.

questions.question: Maya says a mistaken belief about theatre programmes is that

options.text: theatres pay companies to produce them.
few theatre-goers buy them nowadays.
they contain far more adverts than previously.

questions.question: Finn was surprised that, in early British theatre, programmes

options.text: were difficult for audiences to obtain.
were given out free of charge.
were seen as a kind of contract.

questions.question: Maya feels their project should include an explanation of why companies of actors

options.text: promoted their own plays.
performed plays outdoors.
had to tour with their plays.

questions.question: Finn and Maya both think that, compared to nineteenth-century programmes, those from the eighteenth century

options.text: were more original.
were more colourful.
were more informative.

questions.question: Maya doesn't fully understand why, in the twentieth century,

options.text: very few theatre programmes were printed in the USA.
British theatre programmes failed to develop for so long.
theatre programmes in Britain copied fashions from the USA.

questionGroups.instructions: What comment is made about the programme for each of the following shows? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to Questions 27–30.

options.text: Its origin is somewhat controversial.
It is historically significant for a country.
It was effective at attracting audiences.
It is included in a recent project.
It contains insights into the show.
It resembles an artwork.

questions.question: Ruy Blas
Man of La Mancha
The Tragedy of Jane Shore
The Sailors' Festival

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a design and technology lecturer talking about inclusive design.

questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content.text:
**Definition**
Designing products that can be accessed by a diverse range of people without the need for any
Not the same as universal design: that is design for everyone, including catering for people with

content.question: ______ which are adjustable, avoiding back or neck problems
______ in public toilets which are easier to use

content.text:
To assist the elderly:
- designers avoid using
- people can make commands using a mouse, keyboard or their

content.question: commands using a mouse, keyboard or their ______

content.text:
**Impact of non-inclusive designs**
Access:
- Loss of independence for disabled people.
Safety:
- Seatbelts are especially problematic for

content.question: Seatbelts are especially problematic for ______ women

content.text: - PPE jackets are often unsuitable because of the size of women's

content.question: PPE jackets are often unsuitable because of the size of women's ______
PPE for female ______ officers dealing with emergencies is the worst
The ______ in offices is often too low for women
